rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MORTGAGE LENDER SERVICES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: NOTICE OF HEARING REGARDING SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2023 line 1. NOTICE OF HEARING REGARDING SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS is re-noticed for February 28, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CYRUS ESTEBAN ET AL VS. CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend. As to the fraud cause of action Plaintiff shall allege an actionable misrepresentation (e.g. not an offer/solicitation, not an opinion, etc.) against a corporate defendant and damages. Demurrer to the causes of action for TILA violation will be treated as a Motion to Strike and GRANTED. No leave to amend was obtained at any point in the litigation to assert these causes of action. Demurrer to the cause of action for UCL violation is sustained with leave to amend to allege each element of this cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRANDILYNN S. THOMAS VS. JACQUELYNN SMITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Set Aside Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT GLORIA THOMAS' NOTICE OF MOTION, AND MOTION, TO SET ASIDE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is GRANTED. rulings.Case Title: TOM VERA-SWINDLE VS. PARKMERCED OWNER LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And Enter Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2023 line 5. PLAINTIFF TOM VERA-SWINDLE'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AND ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6 is moot. Settlement payment (albeit issued and remitted late) made. Sanctions granted in the amount of $2,405.50. The Court notes that Defendant failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B (courtesy copies of late-filed opposition not received). The Court considered the filing, however, in the future failure to comply with SFLR 2.7B will result in filings not being considered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANSON KWAN VS. MING YOU FENG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 19, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. The Court notes that defendant failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. Failure to comply with SFRL 2.7B in the future by defendant or defendant's counsel will result in motion being denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
